summary: No unit tests exist for ErrorLog by design — user-spec D8 (binding) restricts Phase 1 to exactly one unit test (AllowlistLoader). The decision is defensible for this task: Phase 1 is an explicit throwaway validation spike, ErrorLog's correctness is validated by Task 6 integration (DefaultModelRegistry + DefaultDownloadRepository call e() with real inputs) and Task 14 manual on-device QA (adb shell run-as ... cat files/logs/errors.log reads the actual output), and the implementation is small, deterministic, and produces a human-readable single-line-per-event format that is directly verifiable by the manual channel. No narrow-scope exception to D8 is warranted — the private helpers (sanitize, buildLine) are not exposed, there is no testing infrastructure (Robolectric, androidx.test) in Phase 1 to exercise e() with a real Context, and the short validation chain (Task 6 within the same wave + Task 14 manual read) makes a regression in format or rotation cheap to catch without unit tests. The code as written is testable enough for the defined downstream validation: e() is a straightforward suspend fun injected via Hilt, output is deterministic and catable, rotation preserves the last 2 MB in errors.log.1 (also catable).
